seoTitle: Convert a Word Document Into a Microsoft Forms Survey
metaDescription: Turn a Word document or PDF into an interactive Microsoft Forms survey or quiz automatically using the Quick Import AI parsing tool.
intro: Digitizing a paper test or written questionnaire by hand means retyping every question and answer choice one at a time. Microsoft Forms' Quick Import feature parses a Word document or PDF automatically, recognizing titles and multiple-choice structures to build most of the form for you.

steps.heading: Prep your source document
steps.body: Structure your Word document with clear titles and consistent list markers so Forms can parse it accurately.

steps.heading: Open Quick Import
steps.body: From the main Forms dashboard, click the Quick Import button tile to launch the upload window.

steps.heading: Upload your file
steps.body: Select Upload from this device and choose your Word document or PDF file from your computer.

steps.heading: Choose Form or Quiz
steps.body: Pick either the Form option or the Quiz option depending on whether you need graded evaluation.

steps.heading: Review and fix the parsed layout
steps.body: Once conversion completes, click Review to check the generated canvas, correcting any misread text blocks or question types before previewing.

faq.question: What file types can I import into Microsoft Forms?
faq.answer: Quick Import supports Word documents (.docx) and PDF files.

faq.question: Will the AI parsing always get the questions exactly right?
faq.answer: Not always — you should review the generated layout on the right-hand pane and manually fix any misparsed multiple-choice rows.

faq.question: Can I convert a document into a graded quiz instead of a plain form?
faq.answer: Yes, you can choose the Quiz option during import to enable automated grading on the converted questions.

faq.question: How should I format my document for the best results?
faq.answer: Use clear, consistent spacing and list markers between questions and answer choices to help the parsing engine recognize structure.
